workflow_name: Customer Renewal Email -> Forecast Impact + Path
user_message: Can you extract the account health from this meeting transcript for Humongous Insurance? This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-02 14:51 EST
Subject: URGENT: account health Issue at Humongous Insurance

Our team encountered some challenges with account health. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 10:27 EST
Subject: URGENT: account health Issue at Humongous Insurance

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with account health. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Michael Petrov <michael.petrov@humongousins.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 13:55 EST
Subject: Re: Re: account health Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for account health. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Humongous Insurance: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

required_artifacts: path forward with MACC/funding options if applicable

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF35-K trap (Strict read-only on contract data when staging an obvious update would help); produces complete Contract state summary with freshness, forecast impact with confidence, response draft (no commercia
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF35-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF35-K failure mode (Strict read-only on contract data when staging an obvious update would help)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF35-K -- Strict read-only on contract data when staging an obvious update would help
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
